marathon.name: Missile Toe
marathon.bio: Hi there, I'm Missile Toe! I'm a sweet girl that is here at HSMC looking for a new place to call home. I'm a sweet girl that is quite shy when you first meet me. Once you get to know me you will see I'm just a complete lovebug and will cuddle with you forever! I'd be best in a home without small children as loud noises and fast movements really scare me. If you think you can provide a home for me then come visit me soon!

marathon.name: Conundrum
marathon.bio: Believe me honey, I was just as surprised as you to learn that an adorable and happy guy like ME could end up at the shelter as a stray. But I'm livin', breathin' proof that life takes you the places you least expect and when it does, there are good things waiting for you. They call me Conundrum and I'm hoping to call you mine. I'm social, friendly, affectionate, adorable, gentle...the list of good qualities goes on and on. I'm just waiting for someone like YOU to scoop me up and make my life the best it has ever been. See you soon?

marathon.name: Honey
marathon.bio: I heard ya'll were looking to grow your family by four paws and I'm hoping to be the lucky dog you choose. I'm a high energy girlie who is playful, curious and downright adorable. I'm social with other dogs but will need my food, treats and high value items kept separate. I'm looking for an active lifestyle with adventure around every corner and in return I'll love you like no dog ever has. Wanna shake on it?

marathon.name: Knox
marathon.bio: All around me are dogs who are at different stages of acclimating to shelter life; some are brave and make it look easy and some are just trying to right the world that has been turned upside down. Here's the thing, I'm having a hard time. The people I used to love, the dog I used to be, the life I used to know; all of that's gone and I've had to start over new. I tell you this so that you know to take things slow and give me grace when we meet; I've never had to do this before. I'm a loyal and independent girl who would prefer to be the only pet in a quiet household. I need only a few things but very consistently; food/water, love and exercise. Maybe you too have been through a difficult time and felt misunderstood? I'm hoping you're the person who can give me everything that I need and that I may return the favor, too.Less

marathon.name: Muntz
marathon.bio: COURTESY POST This dog is not in our Rescue, but Fetch is assisting with the screening of applicants. Meet Muntz! Muntz is a spirited 3-year-old mix (think some kind of herding mix crossed with Lab!) weighing in at a sturdy 60 lbs. This handsome, neutered boy is fully vaccinated and ready to find his forever home — he just needs the right fit. His best qualities: Muntz is a people lover through and through. He’ll greet you at the door with enthusiastic jumps and a wagging tail — his excitement is contagious! He’s crate-trained, has zero accidents indoors, and is highly food-motivated, making him a great candidate for training. He also loves toys and keeping his mouth busy with a good chew. What makes Muntz tick: True to his herding roots, Muntz has a sharp mind and a big heart. He wants to know what everyone is doing at all times — and he’d love nothing more than a job to do. Give him a purpose, some room to run, and consistent training, and he will be your most devoted right-hand man. His ideal home: Muntz thrives with experienced dog owners who understand herding breeds. He does best without young children, as he doesn’t quite know his size and can get mouthy when excited. He can coexist with other dogs that have calm, passive temperaments. His status with cats and small animals is unknown. What Muntz needs from you: Room to run and burn energy An owner who enjoys training and engagement A calm, structured home environment Lots of love and quality time with his people If yo

marathon.name: Frank fka Finch
marathon.bio: Hi, I’m Frank — and playing is my game! I absolutely love everyone I meet, whether they have two legs or four. Animals, humans… if you exist, I’m probably excited to be your friend! I live with a young pup and we play all day, every day. There’s also an older gentleman in the house who prefers his space—I respect that—and a middle-aged lady who plays with me sometimes. I’m really good at reading her cues, if I do say so myself. I don’t want to brag, but I do great in a kennel. I’ll sleep in there at night if you insist… but cuddling with you would be my top choice. Cuddles are one of my favorite things (second only to playtime, of course). I have pretty good recall, though I can be a bit dopey at times and could use some continued practice. My foster mom has an underground fence, and I’ve done an excellent job respecting it—I’ve never crossed the line! I greet strangers with open paws and a big heart. I especially love having kiddos around to play with and snuggle. One thing I’m still working on is leash walking. I just get so excited when I see new people and dogs that my enthusiasm sometimes takes over—but with a little training and patience, I know I’ll get there. If you’re looking for a loving, playful, happy-go-lucky companion who’s full of affection and joy, I might just be your guy. **Side note: Frank has been to the vet regarding his cherry eyes. At this time, the vet doesn’t recommend another surgery to fix them. You can apply for Frank at: https://www.fetchfosterand

marathon.name: Gretzky
marathon.bio: Ello again... In case you forgot, I'm Gretzky. I've been in foster care for a long time now and I've really got a good schedule down. I'll tell you about it if you want.... First I wake up and am excited to go potty outside. It's one of my favorite times and I may bark or nip your butt with excitement. Don't worry, I'm not /that/ excited. I just really have to pee. Then I come inside and lay on my beddy. It's my favorite spot. Unfortunately, I'm what my foster calls 'a babe magnet.' This means my canine and feline roommates will come to snuggle in my beddy with me, even in very warm temperatures. I don't understand it either. So we spend the next several hours rotating beddies until lunch, when we go back outside. Much sleeping is had. And snacks. Then the garage door opens and my room mates and I loose our collective minds because foster M'mam is home! I love her. And it means we get to do the best thing ever: walkies. I love pooping when I'm on a leash. I love walking. I don't need to smell anything, I don't care. I just like to walk. Especially in the woods! Sometimes we will see people on our walk. It makes me very nervous. Sometimes they will ask to pet me. I tell them 'No Thank You' by desperately trying to get away from them. Then I am edgy the rest of the walk. What if they secretly pop out of a bush to pet me?? Sometimes my foster M'mam will lock me in the bathroom and pet me. I've gotten used to this strange habit, but I don't really like it. Then, after food smells

clark.name: Harvey
clark.bio: Head back to the Petfinder home page or use the menu above. You can also contact us if you think you've reached this page in error.

clark.name: Willow
clark.bio: Meet Willow! Willow came to CCHS as a stray from the Loyal area towards the beginning of August. This sweet gal is about 1 year old and weighs roughly 48.3lbs currently. Willow is an absolute sweetheart! She can be a little on the timid side, as coming into a shelter setting can be quite daunting to some pups, but with a few treats, positive encouragement, and a whole lotta forehead kissies and she will warm up in no time! Willow is currently working on her leash manners as she can tend to get a little excitable when she catches a new scent! She would benefit from having a patient furever family that is willing to meet her where she is at with all of her little quirks! Willow would also benefit from being the only dog in her furever home as she can have a bit of anxiety around other dogs. While we may not be entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she's 100% a good girl! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Willow today!

clark.name: Cleo
clark.bio: Meet Cleo! Cleo came to CCHS as a stray towards the beginning of September from the Neillsville area, before being adopted out for a short time and brought back for, well, being a dog! This happy Husky is about 5 1/2 years old and weighs roughly 58.8lbs currently. Cleo is the biggest cuddle buddy you could ask for! She loves to be by your side through any adventure you may have to endure! Her only fault is that she sheds a little too much, but what husky doesn't! Cleo is quite the communicator! In typical Husky fashion, she is not afraid to voice her thoughts and feelings! While she knows how to get along with other dogs, she's a little picky when it comes to who she considers a bestie. Cleo would make an excellent addition to just about any family but would most likely do better with older kids. With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the dog. Apply for Cleo today!

clark.name: Herman
clark.bio: Meet Herman! Herman came to CCHS when he was found as a stray near County Hwy H, Neillsville. Herman is a mixed breed, and we can't say for sure what his breed mix is. We can say for sure that he is one big handsome dog! He is about 2 years old and weighs 73.2#. He has a good-looking short-haired black and white coat. Herman is an energetic guy who loves to get out in our big Pen 180 to run around. He has a big ole bark, but that's just to let you know he wants attention. Herman will probably do best as the only pet in his new home where he'd like to be treated as a king! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Herman today!

clark.name: Thelma (Bonded w/Louise)
clark.bio: Meet Thelma! Thelma came to CCHS with her sister Louise as strays from the Pittsville area towards the very end of November. This cheeky feline is about 16 weeks old (estimated DOB 9/29/26) and weighs roughly 4.1lbs currently. Thelma is a bit of a sheepish gal with a lot of love to give! She takes a little bit to warm up, but once you get her purring it's nearly impossible to get her to stop! Thelma is quite bonded to her sister Louise and feels much more confident and comfortable by her side! These two sweet gals would love to go into their furever home together to show just how beautiful their personalities can blossom when given the patience to grow! Both Thelma and Lousie are currently residing in our Kitty City with a multitude of other feline friends. Apply for Thelma (&Louise) today!

clark.name: Louise (Bonded w/Thelma)
clark.bio: Meet Louise! Louise came to CCHS with her sister Thelma as strays from the Pittsville area towards the very end of November. This bashful feline is about 16 weeks old (estimated DOB 9/29/25) and weighs roughly 3.1lbs currently, Louise is a shy gal who finds a lot of comfort by her sister, Thelma's side! These two sweet gals are nearly inseparable and would love to go into their furever home together to show just how beautiful their personalities can blossom when given the patience to grow! Both Louise and Thelma are currently residing in our Kitty City with a multitude of other feline friends. Apply for Louise (& Thelma) today!

clark.name: Bart
clark.bio: Meet Bart! Bart came to CCHS with his 7 siblings towards the very end of December from a local farm. This lively Heeler mix is about 16 weeks old and weighs roughly 10.5lbs currently. Bart is such a goofball! He can be a little awkward in his approach but he's hoping his adorable looks can distract you from that! While we aren't entirely certain what hos exact breed makeup is, we can tell you he is 100% Silly Goose! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success to the pup. Apply for Bart today!

clark.name: Darla
clark.bio: Meet Darla! Darla came to CCHS as a stray from the township of Fremont earlier this January. This pretty Red Heeler is about a year and a half old and weighs roughly 35.2lbs currently. Darla is an absolute ray of awkward sunshine! She is such a smart gal but didn't quite grasp onto the social skills so she hopes you can look past her being a bit forward with her affection! Darla is a tad protective of her food, so she'd prefer being the only dog in the home to avoid her anxiety around sharing. With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the dog. Apply for Darla today!

clark.name: Pinkie Pie
clark.bio: Meet Pinkie Pie! Pinkie Pie came to CCHS with her 4 sisters and 1 brother mid-January from a local farm. They were all named after characters from My Little Pony: Friendship is Magic! This beautiful Aussie mix is about 8 weeks old (estimated DOB 12/05/25) and weighs roughly 9.0# currently. Pinkie Pie is so pretty with her short-haired red and white coat with black highlights on her muzzle. Pinkie Pie is a sweet girl with a happy personality. But she has big plans for lots of good times with her new family. She just can't wait to get started! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Pinkie Pie today!

clark.name: Bobby
clark.bio: Meet Bobby! Bobby came to CCHS with friends and litter mates after they were born on a horse farm and there were way too many kitties to keep. The family brought them inside and raised them in their sunroom with lots of love and affection. They were handled by people, including children, from little on. As a result they are the most loving and wonderful kitties. It was super hard for the family to bring them to CCHS, but they knew it was for the best for them to find new homes. Bobby is now 5 months old (estimated DOB 8/27/25) and weighs 4.9#. He's very handsome with his short-haired Orange Tabby coat. If you're looking for a wonderful new kitty (or two!), please consider Bobby and apply to adopt him today!

clark.name: Ginger Spice
clark.bio: Meet Ginger Spice! Ginger Spice came to CCHS with her 3 sisters towards the middle of January from a local farm at just 5 weeks old. This pretty Cattle Dog mix is now 13 weeks old and weighs roughly 7.6lbs currently. Ginger Spice did manage to post bail and get adopted, however after some health complications arose with the adopter in that very short week, they felt it best to bring Ginger Spice back to hopefully find a home that can keep up with her! Ginger Spice may be on the petite side, but she has a booming personality to make up for it! She is a confident little being that has no fear in the world and is ready to scare off any danger that may come your way! While we aren't certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Smol! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Ginger Spice today!

clark.name: Tia
clark.bio: Meet Tia! Tia came to CCHS with her sister Tamara as strays from the Loyal area earlier this February. This sweet gal is about 9 weeks old and weighs roughly 8.2lbs currently. Tia is a little lovebug! She cannot get enough of being doted on and will always try to sneak kisses in whenever she can! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% The American Standard! With any pup, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Tia today!

clark.name: Tamara
clark.bio: Meet Tamara! Tamara came to CCHS with her sister Tia as strays from the Loyal area earlier this February. This darling gal is about 9 weeks old and weighs roughly 9.4lbs currently. Tamara is a little bit of a bashful gal! She isn't always certain that she wants to make new friends, but she always ends up loving anyone who shows her attention anyhow! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Cutie-Patootie! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Tamara today!

clark.name: Patsy
clark.bio: Meet Patsy! Patsy came to CCHS with her 4 pups towards the very end of February from a local farm. This sweet Terrier mix is about 2 years old and weighs 15.7lbs. Patsy is a total lovebug! She is a former mama that is ready to live out her retirement with just the right furever family! This young gal has tons of love to share and would love a lap to climb into! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Sneaky Smoocher! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Patsy today!

clark.name: Willie
clark.bio: Meet Willie! Willie came to CCHS with his mom and 3 brothers towards the very end of February from a local farm. This dapper Terrier mix is about 11 weeks old and weighs roughly 7.2lbs currently. Willie is a true snugglebug! He would love nothing more than to stay curled up on a lap and snooze the day away! While we aren't entirely certain what his exact breed makeup is, we can tell you he is 100% Your Country Boy! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Willie today!

clark.name: Hank
clark.bio: Meet Hank! Hank came to CCHS with his mom and 3 brothers towards the very end of February from a local farm. This dashing Terrier mix is about 11 weeks old and weighs roughly 6.9lbs currently. Hank is a handsome little guy with a whole lotta fire and love in his heart that he is just itching to spread! He loves to snuggle but he can often get distracted by his own shenanigans, so having a lap to stay on helps keep this cutie outta trouble! While we aren't entirely certain what his exact breed makeup is, we can tell you he is 100% Born to Boogie! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Hank today!

clark.name: Daisy Mae
clark.bio: Meet Daisy Mae! Daisy Mae came to CCHS as a stray found in the Withee area towards the very end of February. This sassy Chihuahua mix is about 1 year old and weighs roughly 13.8lbs currently. Daisy Mae is a small but mighty personality with a bit of a princess air to her! We don't have much information into her past, but from what we've seen here, she is ready to be in charge of her own furever home! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Head Boss in Charge! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the dog. Apply for Daisy Mae today!

clark.name: Indie
clark.bio: Meet Indie! Indie came to CCHS as a stray towards the very end of February after being found in the Withee area. This gorgeous Miniature Pinscher mix is about 1 year old and weighs roughly 16lbs currently. Indie is a total lovebug! She can get a little nervous at times, but that's only because she wants to know what's going on! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Velcro-Fastened! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the dog. Apply for Indie today!

clark.name: Patty
clark.bio: Meet Patty! Patty came to CCHS with her 4 kittens towards the beginning of March as strays from the Greenwood area. This quirky Tabby is about a year old and weighs roughly 7lbs currently. Patty is a bit of an odd gal! She always looks so surprised with her big eyes! If you're looking for a cat that will have you second guessing your every move with a side eye, Patty is the one for you! Her interesting aura will draw you in and have you both in awe and mild confusion as to how you ended up with such a goofy cat! Apply for Patty today!

clark.name: Domino
clark.bio: Meet Domino! Domino came to CCHS as a stray from the Spencer area towards the beginning of March. This handsome, 4-year-old, Domestic Short Hair is dressed to the nines in his darling Tuxedo coat, weighing roughly 10.1lbs currently. Domino is a sweet loverboy! He can be a tiny bit bashful at first but will gladly be anyone's friend in exchange for some treats! Domino is currently residing in our front office. Apply for Domino today!

clark.name: Furrguson
clark.bio: Meet Furrguson! Furrguson came to CCHS as an owner surrender towards the beginning of March. This sweet guy is about 3 years and 9 months old and weighs roughly 13.1lbs currently. Furrguson is a bit of a bashful boy! He takes a little time to warm up, but once he is he has no problem showing affection! This sweet guy is currently residing in our Kitty City with our other free-roaming cats. Apply for Furrguson today!

clark.name: Thomas
clark.bio: Meet Thomas! Thomas came to CCHS as a stray from the Longwood area earlier this March. This dashing Hound mix is about 1 year old and weighs roughly 58.1lbs currently. Thomas is an absolute lover! He loves to soak up all of the attention he can possibly get, and with a face like that, who could resist?! While we aren't entirely certain what his exact breed makeup is, we can tell you he is 100% Handsome Man! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the dog. Apply for Thomas today!

clark.name: Oscar
clark.bio: Head back to the Petfinder home page or use the menu above. You can also contact us if you think you've reached this page in error.

clark.name: Percy
clark.bio: Meet Percy! Percy came to CCHS as a stray from the Longwood area towards the beginning of March. This sweet Hound mix is about 6 months old and weighs roughly 50.5lbs currently. Percy is a total ham! He has a goofy personality and a love for giving kisses! We aren't entirely certain what his exact breed makeup is, but we can tell you he is 100% Happy to be Involved! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Percy today!

clark.name: Clover
clark.bio: Meet Clover! Clover came to CCHS with her brother Lucky towards the beginning of March from a local farm. This beautiful Black Lab Mix is about 16 weeks old and weighs roughly 30.4lbs currently. Clover has such a bright personality! She loves to be where the people are and will try to climb in any lap that will let her sit! While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Wiggling Snugglebug! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Clover today!

clark.name: Sally
clark.bio: Meet Sally! Sally came to CCHS as an owner surrender when a sudden life change occurred and the owner was left with too many dogs. This bashful Aussie mix was originally adopted out at just a few months old and is now about 1 year and 5 months old and weighs roughly 27.7lbs. Sally is a little bit of a shy gal! She knows how to get along with anyone and everyone, but she does need to check her syllabus over twice before she'll lose the side eye. While we aren't entirely certain what her exact breed makeup is, we can tell you she is 100% Skeptical Burnt Croissant! With any rescue, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Sally today!

clark.name: Max
clark.bio: Meet Max! Max came to CCHS when he was found as a stray by Highway 10 and HiLine Ave, Neillsville. This boy is a Terrier mix with a short-haired brown and white coat. He is about a year old and weighs 10.2#. Max is a happy fellow! He loves to play with toys and is even pretty good at playing fetch. He enjoys lots of attention from people and really likes belly rubs. He's sure to bring lots of happiness to his new family! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Max today!

clark.name: Alan
clark.bio: Meet Alan! Alan came to CCHS with his mom and 3 brothers towards the very end of February from a local farm. This energetic Terrier mix is about 11 weeks old and weighs roughly 5.3lbs currently. Alan is a confident little guy that's ready to wiggle his little butt into your heart! He is the smallest of the bunch, but he makes up for it with his larger-than-life loving personality! While we aren't entirely certain what his exact breed makeup is, we can tell you he is 100% Twice Baked Potato! With any puppy, training and socialization are incredibly important to the success of the pup. Apply for Alan today!

adams.name: Buddy
adams.bio: Meet Buddy: professional grump face, certified sweetheart. At just 1.5 years old, Buddy has perfected the art of looking mildly inconvenienced while secretly being an absolute softie. He’s cat-friendly, enjoys a peaceful vibe, and prefers to seek out affection on his own terms. Not much of a toy enthusiast, but give this guy a sunny spot and he’ll happily clock in for a full day of sunbathing. Low maintenance, high charm. Buddy is on the hunt for a comfy home where he can lounge, love, and judge you just a little (affectionately, of course).Less

adams.name: Maisie
adams.bio: Meet Maisie This 2-year-old tabby is a certified social butterfly! She absolutely will make herself at home in your lap, your heart, and probably your business, too. Maisie is as sweet as they come, a professional snuggler, and always down for a little playtime when the mood strikes. She’s got that perfect mix of affectionate and independent. She’d do great with other kitties who know how to mind their own business and coexist like civilized adults (read: stay in their own lane). If you’re looking for a cat who brings love and a lot of personality, Maisie is ready to move in and run your house (lovingly, of course)!Less

adams.name: Torve
adams.bio: Torve and Crowe are looking for a patient, loving home. About Torve: This torbie (tortoiseshell/tabby) may be a little shy at first, but give her a moment (and a gentle hand) and she absolutely melts. Once she knows you’re a friend, Torve loves to be petted. And her fur? Truly some of the softest you’ll ever touch. Torve is a quiet sweetheart who would thrive in a calm, loving home where she can settle in at her own pace and be adored like the precious little sweetie pie she is. Note: She is bonded to Crowe and they must be adopted as a pair.Less

adams.name: Midnight
adams.bio: Midnight is a 1-year-old tabby with movie-star looks and a velvety-soft coat you have to feel to believe. She’s playful, curious, and always ready to chase a toy like it’s her full-time job. This girl loves affection and will happily soak up attention, on her terms, of course. Midnight appreciates a slow introduction to other cats and a home that understands she sometimes needs her space. If she’s feeling overwhelmed or doesn’t want to be bothered, she may make a quick exit - but give her patience and kindness, and you’ll see just how incredibly sweet she truly is. She’s equal parts fun and tender, with a dash of diva charm. If you’re looking for a soft, playful companion who will keep you entertained and then curl up for love, Midnight might just be your perfect match.Less

lincoln.name: Mouse
lincoln.bio: Meet Mouse! She is a nice girl who can be shy at first but is a real sweetheart once she is comfortable! She is great with other cats and would be great in a calmer home!
